summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering community forum items including zoning complaints, a request to live on property during construction, a lot split delay, and a denied building permit that was later overturned by court. The board extensively discussed the use of American Rescue Plan (ARP) funds for a major paving project, approved a base bid of $936,640 for road paving across six roads, approved a replacement truck body and V-box spreader for the service department, and entered executive session regarding complaints against a public employee.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (June 15-28, 2022) - passed
Motion to approve unpaid leave of absence for Detective Steve Maynard after vacation and compulsory time exhausted - passed
Motion to approve out-of-town training for sergeants in Columbus, OH (September, November, December 2022) at $2,500 each - passed
Motion to approve renewing DocTrack agreement for three years at $6,571.80 - passed
Motion to declare property at 6972 (parcel 11434-2.013) a nuisance - passed
Motion to amend agenda adding items K2, K3, and Q - passed
Motion to adopt amended agenda - passed
Motion to reallocate $294,967.97 from ARP fund line item for road paving - passed
Motion to increase appropriations $50,000 in gas tax fund for road paving - passed
Motion to increase appropriations $50,000 in road and bridge fund for road paving - passed
Motion to approve base bid for 2022-2023 road paving awarded to Fillmore Construction LLC at $936,640 (on or after July 1, 2022) - passed
Motion to authorize Township Administrator Steve Pegram to enter into contract with Fillmore Construction LLC - passed
Motion to increase appropriations in gas tax fund $36,517 for truck body replacement - passed
Motion to approve purchase and installation of replacement steel platform body and V-box spreader at $36,517 from W.A. Jones Truck Bodies - passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session for investigation of complaints against public employee - passed
No decisions made from executive session
Motion to adjourn at 9:54 PM - passed
action_items: Steve Pegram to coordinate meeting between Chad (service director), Harry, and Drees Homes (Mark Linger) regarding Villages of Belmont road condition responsibility (Condition #8)
Steve Pegram and Harry to schedule meetings with community members who raised zoning concerns (Andrea Cole, Marie, Scott Humphrey) with a trustee present

Staff to finalize microphone/AV equipment quotes (preliminary quote ~$10,000)
Advertise and publicize the major road paving project to the community

30% increase in paving costs per cubic yard compared to prior year
notable_topics: Community forum: Library promotion and services (Lisa from Goshen Public Library)
Community forum: Zoning violations complaint at Maple Drive and Abandoned Hill Road
Community forum: Request to live on property in campers during home construction (2439 Woodville Pike)
Community forum: Lot split application delayed in zoning department for two months
Community forum: Building permit denial overturned by Clermont County court (rear-to-rear setback misinterpretation)
ARP (American Rescue Plan) fund project status update and spending priorities
Major 2022-2023 road paving project using OPWC grant and ARP funds
Villages of Belmont Phase 2 - Condition #8 road repair responsibility dispute with Drees Homes
Service department plow truck bed replacement with flatbed body and V-box spreader
IRS mileage reimbursement rate mid-year increase to 62.5 cents
Debate over ARP fund allocation priorities (paving vs. electronic sign vs. service garage)
Clermont County Park District improvements to township park (paved entry and parking lot)
National Night Out scheduled for first Wednesday in August at Mark Cook School campus
Executive session for investigation of complaints against public employee
Steve Pegram completed three-week Harvard program
attendees: Chief/Police representative (unnamed, presented police department items)
Marie (resident, 2021 Woodville Pike, lot split issue)
Andrea Cole (property owner, building permit dispute)
Detective Steve Maynard (leave of absence approved)
Mr. Holbrook (referenced, former zoning director who denied permit)
John Phillips (referenced, attorney for Andrea Cole)
Dustin Horn (signed in but on actual agenda, not community forum)
notes: Multiple residents expressed frustration with the zoning department's responsiveness and decisions. The Andrea Cole case revealed a building permit denial based on a zoning interpretation later overturned by a judge, causing a 3-year delay. The board had an extensive and somewhat heated debate about how to allocate ARP funds between road paving, an electronic sign, service garage, and other needs. The meeting had no July meeting scheduled, so agenda amendments were made on the spot to handle budget reallocations. Steve Pegram recently completed a program at Harvard. The IRS mid-year mileage rate change was noted as unprecedented in the trustees' experience. Chad (service director) was absent on vacation, complicating several discussions. The board entered executive session regarding complaints against a public employee with no action taken afterward.
